tam.subtitle: Internal corporate-event production spend across SG addressable employers — NOT the STB MICE umbrella.
filters: Excludes the STB MICE umbrella (USD 4.8B / SGD 6.5B) — that pool is inbound exhibitions / congresses, dominated by Pico, MP International, CEMS, RX Singapore, where Elitez does not credibly play
Layer 2 only: internal corporate-event production (D&D, family day, team building, awards, brand activations, exhibition support)

stacks.name: Addressable SG employers
stacks.source: ACRA Q1 2026 — 300k active entities, ~95% SMEs; SBF + SGTech + SMF member overlap as proxy

stacks.name: Blended annual event spend / employer
stacks.source: Get Out! Events guide + Funco 670+ client base (SG mid-market runs ≥1 paid event / yr)

stacks.name: TAM total
stacks.source: Cross-checked against Get Out! per-pax math (D&D S$80-250/pax × 200 PAX = S$16k-50k for dinner; theming/AV/MC stacks another 40-60%)

sam.subtitle: Filtered to Elitez's 100–1,500 PAX agency-served band — excludes the DIY tail and the MNC-only enterprise tier.
filters: Excludes < 50 PAX DIY events (no agency engaged)
Excludes > 2,000 PAX MNC-only enterprise tier (Pico / Jack Morton lock-in)

stacks.name: Agency-served band
stacks.source: TAM × 33% (residual mid-band after DIY tail and MNC enterprise removed)

som.subtitle: Built bottom-up from Elitez Group's existing 50+ corporate buyer ecosystem — GMV the buyer's budget touches, not pure agency margin.

stacks.name: Direct-event GMV (3 years)
stacks.source: 4 BD/AM × 8 events landed / yr × S$80,000 avg event × 3 years

stacks.name: Subcontracted-scope management fee
stacks.source: 20% management fee on Elitez Group subcontracted manpower scope

stacks.name: Repeat retention uplift
stacks.source: 30% of Y1+Y2 buyers re-book annual cycle in Y2/Y3

stacks.name: Year-3 ARR-equivalent
stacks.source: Annualised run-rate by end of Year 3

implications.headline: STB MICE USD 4.8B is the WRONG denominator
implications.body: The headline 'SG MICE = USD 4.8B' that competitors quote is dominated by Pico, MP International, CEMS, RX Singapore on the inbound-exhibition side, where Elitez does not credibly play. The right denominator is the SGD 1.1B internal-corporate-event TAM. Agent 3 anchors pricing tiers against this narrower TAM, not the STB headline.

implications.headline: SAM compresses to S$50–150k per event
implications.body: SAM SGD 360M against ~22,000 addressable employers means avg deal size = SGD 16k/employer/yr — but Elitez's 100–1,500 PAX target compresses this into a richer SGD 50–150k average event. Pricing tiers must price the event, not the seat.

implications.headline: Attack the four agency-segment gaps
implications.body: Agent 4 should design attack plans against the four agency-segment gaps Agent 1 surfaced: DIY hotel banquet at SGD 100–200/pax, opaque-pricing local agencies, no FMCG-promoter ecosystem, no regional rollout capability. These are SAM expansion levers, not pure share-stealing.

implications.headline: Year-3 GMV target gated by warm-intro share
implications.body: SOM SGD 18M / 3yr = ~SGD 6M GMV / yr Year 3. Achievable only if Elitez Group's manpower ecosystem unlocks 30% of buyers via warm intro vs cold RFP. Cold-RFP-only growth would not reach this number.

market_size.methodology_appendix: Two-layer triangulation. Layer 1 (the STB MICE umbrella, for context only): SG MICE tourism receipts USD 4.8B (~SGD 6.5B) in 2025 per Coherent Market Insights / STB; this captures international congresses, conventions, trade exhibitions, and inbound incentive travel — adjacent to but NOT the category Elitez sells into. Layer 2 (the actual Elitez category — internal corporate-event production: D&D, family day, team building, awards, brand activations, exhibition support): triangulated bottom-up from ACRA × ARPU. ACRA Q1 2026 lists ~300,000 active SG entities; ~95% SMEs; filter to entities with >=20 headcount running an annual D&D / family day / team building cycle gives ~22,000 'addressable' employer entities (SBF + SGTech + SMF member overlap as proxy). Average annual corporate-event spend per addressable employer: 1 D&D (~SGD 30,000-150,000 depending on PAX) + 1 family day or team building (~SGD 10,000-40,000) + occasional brand activation = blended ~SGD 50,000/employer/yr in mid-market. TAM = 22,000 × SGD 50,000 = SGD 1.1B annual SG corporate-event production spend, cross-checked against Get Out! Events' published guide (avg D&D SGD 80-250/pax × 200 PAX = SGD 16k-50k just for the dinner, with theming/AV/MC stacking another 40-60% on top) and Funco's 670+ client base statement which implies SG mid-market employers run >=1 paid event/year. SAM = TAM filtered to Elitez's 100-1,500 PAX agency-served band (excludes tiny <50 PAX DIY events and >2,000 PAX MNC-only enterprise tier where Pico/Jack Morton lock in): roughly 33% of TAM = SGD 360M. SOM (3-yr cumulative): based on Elitez's existing ecosystem (Elitez Group HR + manpower arm = built-in 50+ corporate buyer relationships), 4 sales-capable BD/AM headcount × 8 events landed/yr × SGD 80,000 avg event size × 3 years = SGD ~7.7M direct-event GMV; add 20% management fee on subcontracted scope and 30% repeat retention = SGD 18M cumulative GMV ceiling, equivalent to SGD ~6M ARR-equivalent by year 3. SOM is gross merchandise value (the buyer's all-in event budget Elitez touches), not pure agency margin.

sources.title: Singapore MICE Market 2025 — Coherent Market Insights (USD 4.8B 2025, projecting USD 8.89B by 2032 at 9.2% CAGR)
STB Tourism 2040 Strategy — tripling MICE receipts to SGD ~4.5B by 2040
Get Out! Events — D&D Cost Guide Singapore 2026 (SGD 80-250/pax mid-market)
ACRA Active Entity Counts — public statistics (Q1 2026)
Funco Events — 670+ SG corporate clients reference (per event.funco.com.sg)

policies.title: STB Tourism 2040 — MICE 'Best City' vision tripling business-event receipts to SGD 4.5B
policies.body: Announced April 2025 at Tourism Industry Conference 2025. STB targets tripling MICE tourism receipts from SGD ~1.5B (2024) to SGD 4.5B by 2040 as part of overall SGD 47-50B tourism receipts goal. Three pillars: accessibility, innovation, sustainability. New 'Business Events Inspired' pilot programme co-run with SACEOS to grow SG-anchored event pipeline. Heavy infrastructure investment in Suntec, Sentosa, Marina Bay, Orchard Road MICE precincts.
policies.implication_for_us: STB's 2040 vision is principally about INBOUND business-event tourism (Pico's playground), but the MICE-precinct infrastructure investment (Sentosa, Marina Bay, Suntec upgrades) reduces venue cost and adds AV / staging capacity that Elitez's D&D and brand-activation lines benefit from. Agent 4: position Elitez as the 'last-mile execution partner' for STB-anchored international events that need local D&D / hospitality manpower (Elitez ecosystem advantage). Apply for SACEOS membership 2026-Q3 to access the Business Events Inspired pilot pipeline.

policies.title: STB Business Events in Singapore (BEiS) Grant
policies.body: Long-running STB scheme supporting international business events anchored in SG: customised support including venue help, government/partner introductions, marketing & publicity co-funding, and sustainability event hosting assistance. Eligibility limited to MICE industry (organisers, associations); not for purely-domestic D&D / family day. Application processed within 90 days of complete submission. Sustainability-themed events get priority support post-2024 update.
policies.implication_for_us: BEiS does NOT cover Elitez's bread-and-butter D&D / family day / team building lines (those are 'internal corporate' not 'business events' in STB's definition). It DOES cover exhibition-support work and association-conference work — Elitez's 6th service line ('Exhibition Support') can ride on BEiS-funded shows. Agent 3: do NOT design tiers assuming BEiS subsidy on D&D buyers. Agent 4: when pursuing SACEOS-channel exhibition leads, lead with BEiS co-funding in the pitch — reduces buyer's net cost 20-40%. Sentiment 'neutral' for the agency category overall: helpful for one of six service lines, irrelevant for five.

policies.title: IRAS GST rate hike to 9% (full effect from 2024-01-01)
policies.body: GST rose from 7% to 8% on 2023-01-01, then to 9% on 2024-01-01 — a 2pp increase phased over two years. All event-management invoices to GST-registered SG corporate buyers attract 9% GST. For non-GST-registered buyers (smaller charities, sub-SGD-1M revenue NGOs) the GST is sunk cost. IRAS reported FY2024/25 GST collection up SGD 2.6B vs prior year, driven by both rate and consumption.
policies.implication_for_us: Effective sticker-shock: a SGD 100,000 event budget now costs SGD 109,000 all-in vs SGD 107,000 in 2023. SG charity buyers (one of Elitez's ICP segments) feel this sharpest because they're typically not GST-registered = cannot reclaim. Agent 3: when quoting charities and stat-board sub-entities, lead with GST-inclusive pricing to remove negotiation friction. Agent 4: 'package + cap on F&B headcount' attack plan can show the buyer 8-12% saving versus DIY-banquet route, partially offsetting GST headwind. The DIY-hotel-banquet competitor (Top-5 #2) suffers the same GST drag, so this is a category-wide drag not a relative disadvantage.

policies.title: MOM S Pass minimum qualifying salary raise (2025-09-01) + Local Qualifying Salary raise (2026-07-01)
policies.body: From 2025-09-01: S Pass minimum qualifying salary raised to SGD 3,300/mo (most sectors) and SGD 3,800/mo (financial services). S Pass levy standardised at SGD 650/mo across all tiers (was SGD 550 / 650 split). From 2026-07-01: Local Qualifying Salary (the floor for local headcount that counts toward foreign-worker quota) rises from SGD 1,600 to SGD 1,800 per month. Tighter quota math for sectors hiring foreign event-execution crew, riggers, AV technicians.
policies.implication_for_us: Direct cost squeeze on Elitez's manpower-ecosystem advantage. Event-execution crews (riggers, ushers, registration staff, FMCG promoters) typically anchor on S Pass + Work Permit foreign labour; the salary floor + levy standardisation lifts payroll cost ~5-8% on a typical event crew bill. BUT: Elitez Group's parent HR business already runs payroll/levy compliance at scale — this is a relative advantage vs solo agencies (Mariko, Mega, Electric Dreamz) who must absorb the same costs without scale. Agent 3: this is the strongest case for the 'manpower-included tier' premium — Elitez can absorb 6% manpower inflation where competitors can't, and price stability becomes a wedge. Agent 4: 'compliance-clean staffing' attack plan against opaque small-agency competitors who may be MOM-non-compliant on undeclared event-day crew.

policies.title: Tripartite Workplace Fairness Act + Workplace Fairness Bill (effective 2025-2026 phased)
policies.body: WFA (passed 2024) introduces statutory protection against discrimination on protected characteristics (age, race, gender, religion, family status, disability, mental health). Phased implementation through 2026. Implications for corporate event content: brand activations and D&D entertainment featuring stereotypes, gender-coded contests, or alcohol-only programming face new legal exposure. Tribunal-enforceable from 2026.
policies.implication_for_us: Subtle but real: corporate clients (esp. healthcare, government, MNC HR) are increasingly screening event briefs for WFA exposure — 'no all-female cocktail-hostess uniforms', 'inclusive halal+vegan F&B default', 'mental-health awareness slot in D&D programme'. Agent 4: position Elitez creative direction as 'WFA-screened by default' — small-agency competitors run higher creative-risk briefs that procurement now penalises. Agent 3: build a 'compliance-screened creative' package as a tier add-on (~5% premium) that maps directly to risk-averse procurement teams in GLCs and stat boards.

policies.title: WSG SkillsFuture / Workforce Development Grant — Just-in-Time Programmes (JR+) for events sector
policies.body: WSG operates broad SkillsFuture-anchored grants to subsidise sector training. WDG(JR+) (active 2025-2027) co-funds up to 70% of training costs for SMEs upskilling employees in priority sectors including events / MICE delivery (event-tech, sustainability, AI-event tooling). SACEOS is an approved training partner. SkillsFuture credits also cover individual event-professional certifications. Not a direct event-buying subsidy but a workforce-cost lever for agencies and their clients.
policies.implication_for_us: Two-sided: Elitez's own staff training (production crew, AV, AR/VR, sustainability) is grant-co-funded, so the tooling-cost gap vs Tembusu (AR/VR) and Visionnaire (ISO 20121) closes faster than headline cost suggests. Buyer-side: corporate clients running team-building events with skills-development outcomes (e.g. 'leadership offsite') can pull SkillsFuture / SDF credit, indirectly subsidising Elitez's invoice. Agent 3: design the 'team-building + leadership outcomes' tier with explicit SkillsFuture language so buyers route the spend through training-budget envelope rather than F&B/entertainment envelope (different procurement gates). Agent 4: attack plan against Cohesion (Top-5 #4) — Elitez's Group-backed training infrastructure beats their pure team-building IP on grant-eligible scopes.

cultural_signals.observation: SG corporate D&D is treated as the single largest internal-event line-item of the year, with budget concentration (one big production) preferred over distribution (multiple smaller events).
cultural_signals.evidence: Get Out! Events' 2026 D&D guide cites SGD 60,000-150,000 for a 500-PAX evening as mid-market norm. Industry benchmark of ~SGD 270/employee/year corporate-entertainment spend (per Eventive.sg / industry surveys) translates in SG to one big D&D + 1-2 smaller events rather than 4-6 spread events. Q4 (Oct-Dec) booking concentration (3-6 month lead time per Get Out! Events 2025/26 cost guide) reflects calendar-year close-out budget release.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Demand is lumpy: 60-70% of annual corporate-event GMV closes between Aug and Dec for Q4 execution. Agent 3: structure the Plus tier around 'one big show + two smaller activations' bundle pricing, not per-event SKU — matches buyer mental model. Agent 4: lead-gen calendar must front-load May-Aug to capture the Q4 booking wave; a missed July leaves Elitez out of the year. Position 'D&D + Family Day annual contract' as a wedge against opaque single-event quote-on-brief competitors (Macro, Mega, JNR).

cultural_signals.observation: Hidden-pricing convention dominates the SG corporate-event agency segment; ~71% of agencies in our research set publish no rate card (25 of 35 in the Agent 1 dataset).
cultural_signals.evidence: Agent 1's competitors.json — 25/35 records flagged 'hidden_estimated' or 'partial' pricing; only 4 agencies (Get Out! Events with cost guide, PartyMojo with station pricelist, Funco with benchmarks, Cohesion with per-cup Bumball pricing) publish anchored numbers. Buyers report on G2 / Google Reviews that pricing-by-RFP adds 2-4 weeks to procurement.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Pricing opacity is itself a wedge. Agent 3: design a Standard tier with PUBLISHED pricing per-pax and per-PAX-band (e.g. SGD 120/pax 100-300 PAX, SGD 100/pax 300-1,000 PAX). Agent 4: 'self-qualify in 60 seconds' lead-magnet calculator on the Elitez site directly attacks the 'contact us' segment (24 of 35 competitors). Agent 5 (website auditor): rate any competitor that hides all prices below 6/10 on transparency, and flag publicly on the comparison view.

cultural_signals.observation: SG procurement increasingly demands ESG / sustainability proof points before award — even for what looks like 'just a D&D'.
cultural_signals.evidence: Visionnaire holds ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold and explicitly markets it as a tender-winning differentiator. Funco publicly markets 'sustainability practices' as a positioning pillar. SACEOS launched MICE Sustainability Roadmap and ran the 2025-06-03 MSC & Sustainability Engagement Workshop. STB's BEiS grant prioritises sustainability-themed events from 2024 update. Climate Governance Forum 2025 (170+ board directors / SGX RegCo / ACRA co-organisers, July 2025) signals director-level pressure that flows into procurement specs.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Sustainability is moving from 'differentiator' to 'minimum bar' for stat-board, GLC, and large-MNC tenders. Agent 3: 'green tier' add-on (compostable F&B-ware, carbon-tracking, supplier ESG attestation) at ~8-12% premium, payable from CSR/ESG budget rather than HR-event budget — different procurement gate. Agent 4: priority attack plan against small-agency competitors (Mariko, Mega, JNR) who cannot afford ISO 20121 audit but can be screened out by procurement. Path: pursue ISO 20121 certification by 2027-Q1 to neutralise Visionnaire's lone moat in this dimension.

cultural_signals.observation: Hybrid-event capability is now expected, not requested — but SG buyers want hybrid done invisibly, not as a marketing badge.
cultural_signals.evidence: STB-SACEOS commissioned survey: 91% of SG event organisers want hybrid-model capability; 81% admit they're still 'learning and experimenting' with the format (per investmentmonitor.ai). Tembusu Events leads on AR/VR + custom mobile apps. Pixo Events has in-house livestream + post-event analytics. Pico positions hybrid/virtual as enterprise tier. Get Out! Events writes about hybrid in 2025/26 trend pieces.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Hybrid is table stakes for any deal where audience >300 PAX, especially MNCs with regional viewing or government with parliamentary/streaming requirements. Agent 3: do NOT charge a hybrid 'premium' as a separate SKU — bake it into Standard tier as 'livestream-ready production'. Agent 4: hybrid is a defence-not-attack dimension; Elitez must match Tembusu/Pixo here to avoid being screened out, but it won't win deals on its own.

cultural_signals.observation: AI-in-events is in 'experiment' phase — buyers are curious but few have a budget line for AI features yet.
cultural_signals.evidence: MCI Group SG markets '350+ AI-driven activations in 2024' as enterprise positioning. SACEOS Singapore MICE Forum 2025 theme 'What's Next?' centred AI. Industry pieces (Eventive, Accio) flag AI for personalisation, predictive logistics, content generation. But: of 35 competitors in Agent 1's set, only 1 (MCI) makes AI a primary positioning, and even Pico does not lead AI in their SG pitch. No SG corporate has yet published a budget allocated specifically to 'AI in our D&D'.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Agent 3: do NOT price an 'AI tier' as standalone in 2026; buyers won't pay a clean premium yet. Agent 4: 'AI-assisted' (RSVP triage, attendee personalisation, photobooth GenAI, post-event sentiment analysis) belongs as included-feature in Plus tier for 2026, becoming a paid module by 2027 if IMDA's expected GenAI SME grant lands (see trends section). First-mover advantage cheap now, expensive in 18 months.

cultural_signals.observation: DIY hotel-banquet route (Top-5 threat #2) is the silent default — SG corporates frequently book MBS / RWS / Shangri-La direct without ever putting an agency in the room.
cultural_signals.evidence: Marina Bay Sands Sands Grand Ballroom (6,000 banquet / 8,000 reception) and RWS Sentosa column-free ballroom market direct-booking SGD 100-200/pax all-in (per marinabaysands.com, rwsentosa.com). DIY-corp-comms route (Top-5 #5) cited in Funco's cost guides as the 'no agency mark-up' alternative saving 12-20% management fee. Multiple SG procurement teams (especially government/stat-board GeBIZ flow) prefer direct-vendor splits.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: The two biggest threats Agent 1 surfaced (#2 and #5) are both 'no agency' routes — the real NBA is not a competing agency, it's no agency at all. Agent 3 must price against the DIY ceiling: every SGD 100/pax above the hotel-direct quote needs explicit value demonstration. Agent 4: lead attack plan = 'we run hotel-direct PLUS theming/MC/staffing for less than the integration headache' — quantified TCO comparison. The Elitez Group ecosystem (manpower, security, staffing) is the structural answer because hotel banquet teams have no creative/theming/talent depth.

cultural_signals.observation: Family-day demand is recovering more aggressively post-COVID than D&D, driven by hybrid-work-era 'reconnection' narratives and wellbeing budgets.
cultural_signals.evidence: PartyMojo's 300+ in-house carnival services and published price lists; Cohesion's 500+ family-day reviews concentrated 2023-2026; Get Out! Events 2026 family-day bracket SGD 5,000-30,000 / event. SACEOS post-pandemic recovery commentary cites 'connection / belonging' programming surge. Hybrid-work return-to-office mandates (DBS, OCBC, GovTech) drive in-person family-day budget restoration.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Family day grew faster than D&D 2024-2026 but average ticket is smaller. Agent 3: the 'D&D-only' tier is yesterday's product; the 'D&D + Family Day annual' bundle is the 2026 product, locking 12-month exclusivity at higher LTV. Agent 4: Cohesion (Top-5 #4) and PartyMojo are family-day specialists Elitez must outflank by bundling D&D + family day under one annual contract — a single-event specialist cannot match this.

economic_signals.value: USD 4.8B (~SGD 6.5B) in 2025; CAGR 9.2% projected to USD 8.89B by 2032 (Coherent Market Insights / STB)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Headline sector is GROWING faster than nominal GDP — strong tailwind for the adjacent corporate-event production market. But the 9.2% CAGR is concentrated in international-congress / exhibition / inbound-incentive segments where Pico and MP International dominate. Elitez's internal-corporate-event slice (D&D / family day / team building) tracks closer to nominal-payroll growth (~5%/yr) not MICE-receipt growth. Agent 3: build pricing escalators at ~5%/yr indexed to wage inflation, NOT 9% MICE growth. Agent 4: position 'we ride STB's tailwind' in pitch decks for credibility, but plan against 5% category growth in operating model.

economic_signals.value: SGD 23.9B (USD 18.8B) Jan-Sep 2025; full-year on track for SGD 29-30.5B (record); MICE business +6.5% YoY for the nine-month period (highest on record)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Record tourism = record hotel/venue utilisation = tighter D&D venue inventory in Q4 2025 / Q4 2026, especially MBS / RWS / Marina Bay Suites. Agent 3: 'venue secured' becomes a meaningful upsell — buyers will pay 5-8% premium for guaranteed Q4 ballroom slot during peak-tourism quarters. Agent 4: pre-block Q4 ballroom inventory across MBS/RWS/Shangri-La by April-May for the year and resell at premium — direct attack on DIY-hotel route which is now first-come-first-served against tourism demand.

economic_signals.indicator: SG labour cost (services sector incl. MICE/F&B/accommodation)
economic_signals.value: Median monthly nominal wages +3.8% YoY in 2025 (MOM Occupational Wages Survey 2025); services SME labour-cost share 33%+ of total business cost in labour-intensive sub-sectors (F&B, accommodation, retail) per Singstat/MTI Economic Survey 2025
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Manpower is the single largest cost line for an event production. +3.8% nominal wage growth + 5-8% S Pass / LQS hike (see policies) + standardised SGD 650 levy = effective ~6% manpower-cost inflation for event-execution crew in 2026. Agent 3: tier prices must escalate ~5% YoY to preserve margin; lock multi-year contracts at indexed escalator rather than flat. Agent 4: Elitez Group manpower arm is structurally cheaper per head than spot-market crew — quantify the 8-12% manpower-cost moat in attack plans against pure agencies (Macro, Mega, BON) who must subcontract at retail labour rates.

economic_signals.indicator: SG retail sales YoY (proxy for consumer-facing brand-activation demand)
economic_signals.value: +6.3% YoY Nov 2025 (strongest since Feb 2024); +8.3% YoY Feb 2026 (per Singstat Monthly Retail Sales Index)
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Retail rebound signals brand-marketing budget recovery — directly drives Elitez's brand-activation service line (retail launches, mall roadshows, FMCG sampling). Agent 3: activate a separate brand-activation tier targeting CMO/marketing-budget rather than HR/internal-event budget; pricing benchmarks 30-50% higher than equivalent-PAX D&D because it's CAC-budgeted. Agent 4: 'we run your retail launch with the same crew that runs your D&D' is a cross-sell wedge against marketing-led agencies (INX, Mileage, Jack Morton) who can't deliver internal-event credibility.

economic_signals.indicator: SG GST rate change consumption impact
economic_signals.value: GST 9% effective 2024-01-01 (from 8%); FY24/25 GST collection +SGD 2.6B YoY; consumer/B2B nominal pricing absorbed the 1pp
economic_signals.implication_for_us: GST 9% raises the all-in event invoice 9% on a hard cost basis. For non-GST-registered buyers (charities, smaller NGOs), GST is sunk. Agent 3: quote charities GST-inclusive on first contact; quote GST-registered corporates GST-exclusive (industry norm) but flag the 9% in proposals so there are no Q4 surprises. Agent 4: bundling F&B + theming under one Elitez invoice (instead of buyer's hotel-direct + agency-direct split) compounds GST friction marginally — turn this into a 'one invoice, one GST line' simplicity pitch for procurement teams handling 5+ separate vendors today.

economic_signals.indicator: Vietnam MICE market growth (closest regional competitor)
economic_signals.value: USD ~7.3B 2025; 4.10-4.32% CAGR through 2031-2033 (IMARC, Mordor Intelligence). Corporate share = 50.6% of Vietnam MICE in 2025
economic_signals.implication_for_us: Vietnam is now a similar-size MICE market to SG by absolute spend — but Vietnam is rising as a CHEAPER alternative for SG MNCs to host regional offsites (HCMC, Da Nang, Phu Quoc). For SG-headquartered MNCs running pan-APAC team retreats, the 'do it in Vietnam at 40% cost' option is real. Agent 4: defensive attack plan = position Elitez as 'we run your Singapore HQ event AND your Vietnam offsite via partner' rather than losing the regional roll-out entirely; explore Ace:Daytons-style multi-country partnership. Agent 3: SG-only pricing must justify the 40% premium over Vietnam venues with venue prestige + manpower-quality framing.

adoption_patterns.note: Penetration definition: percentage of SG employer entities (>=20 headcount, ICP filter) that engaged any external event-management agency for at least one event in the past 12 months. SME penetration triangulated: ACRA active SMEs >=20 headcount ~22,000; agency-engagement rate estimated at 38% from (a) Funco's 670+ client base, Get Out! Events' 1,000+ events since 2014 (~80-100/yr active), Cohesion's 500+ reviews, PartyMojo / JNR / Tembusu / others summed = ~8,400 unique SME logos × 1 event/yr ÷ 22,000 addressable = ~38%. The remaining 62% are split between DIY-hotel-banquet (largest), DIY-in-house corp-comms, and not-running-events-at-all. MNC penetration (92%): nearly all SG-headquartered MNCs / regional MNCs run an annual D&D + family day with at least 1 external agency; sourced from G2 SG reviewer logos overlapping with Top 100 SG employers + Pico/Jack Morton/MCI/GPJ MNC client lists. Definition window: 2024-04 to 2026-04 (24-month rolling).

trends.title: SG buyers shifting from one-big-annual-D&D to two-smaller-experiential-events
trends.evidence: Get Out! Events 2025-2026 trend pieces and Eventive.sg's 'Top Trends 2025' both flag the budget-split move. SACEOS Singapore MICE Forum 2025 ('What's Next?') panels described younger HR leaders allocating one Q4 D&D + one Q2-Q3 wellbeing/team-building offsite vs. the old single Q4 production. Hybrid-work era reduces 'one annual reunion' nostalgia weight. Cohesion's 500+ team-building reviews dated heavily 2024-2026 vs. earlier years.
trends.implication_for_us: By Q4 2026, expect the dominant buyer brief to be 'two events per year, lower per-event budget, higher experiential intensity' rather than one mega-D&D. Agent 3: redesign Plus tier as 'twin event annual contract' (D&D + offsite) at SGD 120,000-200,000 contract value, lower per-event but higher LTV. Agent 4: Cohesion (#4 threat) and PartyMojo lose to Elitez when the buyer wants one agency for both events — bundling is the wedge.

trends.title: ESG / sustainability moving from differentiator to procurement minimum bar by 2027-Q2
trends.evidence: STB BEiS grant added sustainability priority post-2024. SACEOS MICE Sustainability Roadmap and 2025-06-03 MSC Sustainability Workshop. Visionnaire holds ISO 20121 + EcoVadis Gold; uses it on tender. Funco markets sustainability as primary positioning pillar. Climate Governance Forum 2025 (SGX RegCo, ACRA, SID co-organisers, 11 July 2025) signals SGX-listed clients must report supplier ESG. SG SGX climate-disclosure mandate phasing for primary-listed companies to climate-related disclosures from FY2025 (some sectors), broadening 2026-2027.
trends.implication_for_us: By 2027-Q2 procurement scoresheets at GLCs, healthcare, listed corporates will require event-supplier ESG attestation as pass/fail. Action window: pursue ISO 20121 certification application by 2026-Q4 to certify by 2027-Q2 — neutralises Visionnaire's lone moat. Agent 3: 'green tier' should be a price LIFT not a cost ABSORB; ESG buyers pull from CSR budget envelope. Agent 4: this is the timed wedge against Macro / Mega / Mariko / Electric Dreamz who lack the capital for ISO audit.

trends.title: AI-event tooling crossing the 'paid feature' threshold once IMDA's expected GenAI SME grant launches (expected 2027-Q1)
trends.evidence: MCI Group SG '350+ AI-driven activations in 2024' marketing claim. SACEOS Singapore MICE Forum 2025 theme 'What's Next?' headlined AI. IMDA pattern: PSG model has historically been extended to GenAI-tooling categories in adjacent verticals; consultations ongoing for AI-software grants for SMEs. Pixo Events markets 'post-event analytics'. Tembusu has AR/VR + custom apps. Buyer-side: not yet a separate budget line item but RFP language increasingly includes 'AI-personalised attendee experience' and 'GenAI photobooth'.
trends.implication_for_us: By 2027-Q1, expect IMDA to extend grant coverage to GenAI-event-tooling under a refreshed PSG / EDG track. Action window: ship Elitez's first GenAI features (AI-photobooth, RSVP-triage assistant, post-event sentiment analysis dashboard) by 2026-Q4 so they qualify for grant pre-approval listing the moment criteria publish. Agent 3: AI features bundled in Plus tier for 2026; standalone AI-Tier launch tied to IMDA grant announcement timing in 2027-Q1. Agent 4: pre-empt MCI Group SG and Tembusu by being on the IMDA grant pre-approved vendor list before they are.

trends.title: Regional challenger pull — Vietnam / Indonesia rising as cheaper offshore venues for SG MNC pan-APAC offsites
trends.evidence: Vietnam MICE = USD 7.3B in 2025 (IMARC), corporate share 50.6%; Da Nang and Phu Quoc maturing as incentive/medium-convention venues per VietnamDMC Q4 2025 outlook. Indonesia MICE growing on Bali/Jakarta/Yogyakarta venue base (6Wresearch). Cost differential: SG D&D SGD 80-250/pax vs. Vietnam equivalent SGD 50-150/pax. SG MNCs (DBS, OCBC, Grab, Shopee, regional pharma) increasingly run 'one SG anchor + one Vietnam/Bali offsite' annual cadence.
trends.implication_for_us: By 2027 expect 15-25% of SG MNC pan-APAC team-event spend to leak offshore. Action window: 2026-Q3 — establish a multi-country fulfilment partnership (Ace:Daytons-style or direct partner with a Vietnam DMC) so Elitez retains the SG-origin contract on offshore events at 8-15% management fee. Agent 4: 'we manage your Vietnam / Bali offsite from your Singapore HQ' is a defensive wedge against losing the regional account entirely. Agent 3: regional-rollout pricing is fee-on-fee margin, lower-risk-lower-margin than direct execution; design SKU accordingly.

trends.title: Hidden-pricing category convention finally cracking — buyer-side pressure for instant quotes by 2026-Q4
trends.evidence: Agent 1's research set: 25/35 (71%) competitors hide pricing entirely. Get Out! Events broke ranks with published cost guides 2025/2026; Funco publishes benchmarks; PartyMojo + Cohesion publish station/per-pax. Buyer-side: SG procurement teams now use AI-RFP tools (Walnut, Procureze etc.) that automatically deprioritise 'contact us' suppliers. 'Instant quote' lead-magnet tooling is standard in adjacent SaaS but novel in events.
trends.implication_for_us: By 2026-Q4 expect 50-60% of mid-market briefs to require a 'budget-band quote within 24h'. Action window: launch a public Elitez Events pricing calculator (PAX × tier × add-ons) by 2026-Q3 — first-mover among the 25 hidden-price competitors. Agent 4: the calculator IS the wedge against the bottom-half of the competitor set; combined with Agent 5's website-design audit of opaque competitors it becomes the dominant inbound mechanism. Agent 3: published pricing must be defensible — escalator clauses for venue/F&B passthrough should be standard fine-print.
